---
title: "high-risk situations map"
format: html
---

## High-Risk Situations Map

Some moments increase the chance of drinking because pressure goes up while coping capacity goes down. These moments can be external (certain places, people, events) or internal (stress, anger, shame, loneliness, overconfidence, exhaustion).  

Relapse risk usually builds in stages. It often starts with cues, then permission-giving thoughts, then emotional escalation, then behavior that moves closer to alcohol. Mapping your personal pattern helps you intervene early.

Recovery is strengthened by planning ahead, not relying on willpower alone in high-pressure moments. Many setbacks follow predictable patterns: rising stress, narrowing options, and automatic decisions that increase risk.

Structured planning interrupts that chain. By identifying risk earlier and choosing concrete responses, you reduce decision fatigue and increase the chance of staying aligned with your goals.

::: callout
### Instructions
Use recent examples. Be specific about where, when, who was present, and what you told yourself.
:::

| Situation (where/when/who) | Trigger thoughts and feelings | Risk level (1-10) | Protective action |
|---|---|---|---|
| | | | |
| | | | |
| | | | |
| | | | |

### Early Warning Signs

- Body signs:
- Thought signs:
- Behavior signs:
